pr.title: Add some defines for now to document Peach's items
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced raw item-kind numeric constants in Peach's OnLoad item registration with named preprocessor defines in ftpeach.h. The change documents Peach-specific item IDs used by func_8026B3F8 without altering behavior.
what_changed: Added Peach item ID defines to src/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.h: ITEM_PEACH_UNK 98, ITEM_PEACH_TURNIP 99, ITEM_PEACH_PARASOL 103, ITEM_PEACH_TOAD 104, and ITEM_PEACH_TOAD_SPORE 111.
Updated ftPeach_OnLoad in src/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.c to pass those named defines to func_8026B3F8 instead of raw constants 98, 99, 103, 104, and 111.
No functional logic appears to have changed; the diff is a small readability/documentation pass over existing item registrations.
smart_moves: Converted unexplained magic numbers into named constants at the local character header level, making later Peach item work easier to search and reason about.
Kept the numeric values unchanged, reducing behavioral risk while improving documentation.
Used names that encode both the owning character and item identity, which helps distinguish Peach-specific item IDs from generic item constants.
decomp_lessons: When exact enum placement or broader item taxonomy is not yet settled, local #define names can still document known IDs and remove magic numbers from matched C code.
Small non-behavioral documentation changes are useful in decompilation because they preserve matching while making later symbol recovery easier.
Character OnLoad item registration calls are good candidates for replacing raw item-kind IDs with searchable names once the item identity is known.
naming_conventions: Peach item constants were named with the prefix ITEM_PEACH_.
Known item identities used descriptive uppercase suffixes: TURNIP, PARASOL, TOAD, TOAD_SPORE.
An unknown Peach item retained uncertainty explicitly as ITEM_PEACH_UNK rather than inventing an unverified name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Maintained the same literal integer values behind preprocessor defines, so compiled output should remain equivalent while source readability improves.
This is a magic-number-to-define cleanup rather than a control-flow or struct-layout matching tactic.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for whether ITEM_PEACH_UNK was identified and renamed.
Search for func_8026B3F8 call sites to see whether other fighters had item IDs converted from raw constants to named defines or enums.
Investigate whether these ITEM_PEACH_* defines were later moved into a central item enum/header.
